title: NamedView Object
description: Represents a named view as seen in the browser.

methods.name: apply
methods.description: This updates the active viewport to use the camera associated with this named view.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes this named view. This method will fail for any of the four standard named views. This can be determined by checking to see if the isBuiltIn property is true.

properties.name: camera
properties.description: Gets and sets the camera associated with this named view. This property acts as read-only for the four standard named views. This can be determined by checking to see if the isBuiltIn property is true.

properties.name: isBuiltIn
properties.description: Indicates if this named view is one of the four standard named views ("TOP", "FRONT", "RIGHT", and "HOME"). There is limited functionality with the four standard named views.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of this named view. This property acts as read-only for the four standard named views. This can be determined by checking to see if the isBuiltIn property is true.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentProduct
properties.description: Returns the parent product of this named view.

apply.title: NamedView.apply Method
apply.description: This updates the active viewport to use the camera associated with this named view.
apply.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

classType.title: NamedView.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: NamedView.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes this named view. This method will fail for any of the four standard named views. This can be determined by checking to see if the isBuiltIn property is true.

camera.title: NamedView.camera Property
camera.description: Gets and sets the camera associated with this named view. This property acts as read-only for the four standard named views. This can be determined by checking to see if the isBuiltIn property is true.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = namedView_var.camera

# Set the value of the property.
namedView_var.camera = propertyValue

isBuiltIn.title: NamedView.isBuiltIn Property
isBuiltIn.description: Indicates if this named view is one of the four standard named views ("TOP", "FRONT", "RIGHT", and "HOME"). There is limited functionality with the four standard named views.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = namedView_var.isBuiltIn

isValid.title: NamedView.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = namedView_var.isValid

name.title: NamedView.name Property
name.description: Gets and sets the name of this named view. This property acts as read-only for the four standard named views. This can be determined by checking to see if the isBuiltIn property is true.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = namedView_var.name

# Set the value of the property.
namedView_var.name = propertyValue

objectType.title: NamedView.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = namedView_var.objectType

parentProduct.title: NamedView.parentProduct Property
parentProduct.description: Returns the parent product of this named view.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = namedView_var.parentProduct
